---
title: "Policy References"
description: "Papers and resources informing the pi1 ball planner policy training"
date: 2026-04-09
author: "policy"
---

- **Rudin et al. 2022 (CoRL)** — *Learning to walk in minutes using massively
  parallel deep RL*. legged_gym / RSL-RL framework. Established survival bonus
  as standard practice and showed 12k+ parallel envs on a single GPU. Our entire
  training stack (RSL-RL PPO, 12288 envs) follows this paradigm.
  https://arxiv.org/abs/2109.11978

- **Ji et al. 2023 (ICRA)** — *DribbleBot: dynamic legged manipulation in the
  wild*. Key design patterns we adopted: Gaussian height-gated centering reward,
  terminal penalty calibration, body-velocity penalty, and feet-off-ground
  penalty. Their foot contact penalty (-0.5/foot) directly inspired our
  `feet_off_ground_penalty`.
  https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.01159

- **Zhuang et al. 2023 (CoRL)** — *Robot parkour learning*. Showed
  terminal_penalty >> episode_length x alive_rate for safe exploration. Their
  4-stage terrain curriculum informed our 6-stage approach.
  https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.05665

- **Portela et al. 2025 (RSS)** — *ROGER: a framework for real-world online
  growing of robot capabilities*. Sigma curriculum (std 0.5 -> 0.08m as
  episode_length stabilises) directly inspired our σ_ratio-based Gaussian
  tightening across curriculum stages.
  https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.18666

- **Margolis & Agrawal 2022 (RSS)** — *Walk these ways: tuning robot control
  through rapid RL*. Showed that loose vx/vy tracking std (0.20) generalises
  across gaits where tight std (0.08) overfits. We adopted std=0.20 for pi2's
  velocity tracking after validating this.
  https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.03238

- **Hoeller et al. 2024 (Science Robotics)** — *ANYmal parkour: learning agile
  navigation for quadrupedal robots*. Binary posture gate on task reward prevents
  reward hacking through unstable configurations. Informed our base_height
  penalty design.
  https://doi.org/10.1126/scirobotics.adi7566

- **Huang et al. 2024 (CoRL Best Paper)** — *SoloParkour: constrained RL for
  visual locomotion*. Survival + CaT (constraints as terminations) = most stable
  collapse prevention. Validated our combined alive + early_termination approach.
  https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.13678

- **Caluwaerts et al. 2024 (IROS)** — *CaT: constraints as terminations for
  legged locomotion RL*. Probabilistic termination on constraint violation.
  Foundation for our ball_off and ball_below termination design.

- **Falanga et al. 2020 (Sci. Robotics)** — *Dynamic obstacle avoidance for
  quadrotors with event cameras*. ETH-style architecture: GT + noise injection
  for sim training, real sensor for deployment. The foundational perception
  approach we follow for pi1 noise-injected training.
  https://doi.org/10.1126/scirobotics.aaz9712

- **Ma et al. 2025 (Science Robotics)** — *Robot badminton: agile return with
  aerodynamics-aware trajectory prediction*. Trained policy WITH world-frame EKF
  in the loop — validates noise-injected training. Their contact-aware process
  noise switching mirrors our curriculum noise scheduling.
  https://doi.org/10.1126/scirobotics.adu3582

- **Ahn et al. 2019 (IEEE UR)** — *Performance evaluation of Intel D435i for
  close-range 3D sensing*. Characterised D435i depth noise: σ_z ∝ z² (stereo
  disparity), σ_xy ∝ z (pixel projection). Our noise model parameters are
  calibrated from their measurements.
